title: DerivedDistributions
description: Empirical event-rate distributions for an industry sim. Loaded by sim handlers at bootstrap and used as Poisson rates / categorical probabilities for event scheduling. Sources distinguish provenance: 'placeholder' (hand-authored realistic values; ships before ingestion pipeline runs) vs 'statsbomb-open-data' / 'cricsheet' / etc. (computed by per-industry ingestion loaders in polis-internal/app/retrieval/ingestion/). The shape is industry-agnostic — football declares goals/shots/fouls; basketball would declare shots/fouls/turnovers; manufacturing would declare defect-rates/throughput. Per V0-PLAN §3.10 + DECISION-SIM-ENGINE.md (open kernel + per-industry sim configs).

source_id.description: Slug identifying the source. 'placeholder' for hand-authored; otherwise matches a polis/configs/sources.yaml entry (e.g. 'statsbomb-open-data', 'cricsheet'). Operator pipeline replaces placeholder file with real source_id once ingestion runs.

provenance.description: How the numbers got here. 'placeholder' = hand-authored before ingestion pipeline runs (clearly tagged so contributors can't mistake them for real data). 'computed' = output of an ingestion loader against a real corpus (statsbomb-open-data, kloppy, etc.). 'manual-review' = computed values that an operator hand-edited (e.g., trimming outliers); requires explanation in metadata.review_notes.

computation_version.description: Semver of the ingestion-pipeline computation logic. Bump when the math changes (e.g., switching from mean to median, new outlier filter). Lets sims pin which computation flavor they were calibrated against.

matches_processed.description: Number of matches / sessions / units the metrics were computed over. 0 for placeholder. Sim calibration tests use this to scale expected variance.

events_processed.description: Total event count across all processed matches. Audit-trail field for spotting truncated ingestion runs.

metrics.description: Industry-defined per-match metrics, each as a {mean, stdev, n} distribution. Handlers convert mean → Poisson rate over 90 min (or relevant duration) for sampling event arrivals. Industry handlers know which metric names to expect; the schema doesn't enforce a fixed vocabulary so this same shape works for football (goals_per_match), basketball (shots_per_quarter), boxing (punches_per_round), etc.

outcomes.description: Categorical outcome distribution. Free-form keys; values must sum to ~1.0. For football: home_win_rate + draw_rate + away_win_rate. For boxing: ko_rate + tko_rate + decision_rate + draw_rate. Optional — only present when the industry has a small fixed outcome categorization.

scorelines_top.description: Top-N most-common scorelines with their empirical frequencies. Football: {home, away, freq}. Calibration target — sim's outcome distribution should approximate these freqs over many runs. Optional; absent for industries without a {home, away} score model.

goal_minutes_distribution.description: Per-minute event-arrival histogram. Football-specific: minute 0..90+ with goal frequency. Handlers can sample by minute bucket for time-of-game realism (e.g., late-game goal density spike). Optional; industries without a time-bucketed arrival model omit it.

metadata.description: Free-form. Used for review_notes (when provenance='manual-review'), ingestion_at timestamp, license review status, calibration tolerance overrides, etc.

mean.description: Sample mean across processed matches. Sim handlers use this as the Poisson rate (per-match) or the categorical probability anchor.

stdev.description: Population standard deviation. 0 when n<=1. Sim calibration tests use mean ± k*stdev/sqrt(N) bands as accept/reject windows.

n.description: Sample size (matches contributing to the statistic). 0 for placeholder; non-zero once ingestion runs.
